title: QUIC Server-Role CertificateVerify Continuation Architecture
purpose: Describe the permanent server-role local CertificateVerify continuation that lives behind the existing transcript-progress and bridge-driver seam, extends the server floor after local ServerHello, Handshake key availability, local EncryptedExtensions, and local Certificate, accepts a narrow explicit raw signing-key seam compatible with the supported raw-DER leaf certificate seam, appends one local CertificateVerify message, and exposes outbound CertificateVerify bytes through the existing library-owned handshake seam without claiming server Finished or interop support.
design_summary: The connection library keeps server-role local CertificateVerify emission as the next bridge-owned slice after the existing server Certificate continuation rather than adding a second TLS stack or a credential subsystem. The existing transcript-progress owner remains responsible for ordered Handshake CRYPTO assembly, deterministic message framing checks, and peer transport-parameter staging from ClientHello. Once that owner has accepted a supported ClientHello for TLS 1.3 with TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 over secp256r1, the managed key-schedule owner has already published the local ServerHello, Handshake key availability, the local EncryptedExtensions continuation, and the local Certificate continuation, and the server-local signing seam carries raw P-256 material that is compatible with the previously supplied raw-DER leaf certificate, the key schedule formats one deterministic local CertificateVerify message for the supported server verification context, appends that message behind the local Certificate at the transcript boundary, and exposes the local CertificateVerify bytes as Handshake-level CryptoDataAvailable with the correct offset semantics on the existing handshake seam. The slice deliberately stops after local CertificateVerify and does not synthesize server Finished, commit, endpoint-host wiring, or interop-runner handshake support.
risks: The server-role signing seam must stay narrow and must not grow into a broad credential-management subsystem or a platform certificate-store integration.
The supported subset must stay pinned to TLS 1.3 with TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 over secp256r1 until a later requirement explicitly widens it.
Outbound CertificateVerify availability must not be mistaken for server Finished ownership, handshake completion, or interop support.

Server-role commit must remain blocked even though Handshake keys become available at the ClientHello plus ServerHello transcript boundary, local EncryptedExtensions is emitted on the existing handshake seam, local Certificate is emitted on the existing handshake seam, and local CertificateVerify is emitted on the existing handshake seam.
